## Top 3 root-cause classes (acceptance criterion)

### 1. cv2 style-attribute lift gap — 944 of 1102 entries (86%)

944 entries have `source: cv2_emitted_dynamic` — cv2 successfully recognised the block and EMITTED it, but failed to extract values for attributes that should have been lifted. This is the **lift gap** Phase 3 P-tickets target.

**Top slot families failing to lift** (style/typography/border attributes):

| Slot family | Count | Phase 3 ticket that fixes it |
|-------------|-------|------------------------------|
| `letterSpacing`, `textTransform` | 18+18 | P-PARENT-QUALIFIED-TAG-LIFT (3.1) + P-TAG-SELECTOR-LIFT (3.2) — tag-only typography rules |
| `hoverScale` | 16 | P-PARENT-QUALIFIED-TAG-LIFT — hover state lift |
| `backgroundColour` | 13 | P-PARENT-QUALIFIED-TAG-LIFT |
| `fontFamily`, `fontSizeMobile`, `fontSizeTablet`, `letterSpacingUnit`, `textDecoration` | 12 each | P-CORE-STYLE-MAP-DB-MIGRATION (3.3) — typography path map |
| `borderRadius{TL,TR,BL,BR}`, `boxShadow` | 11 each | P-PARENT-QUALIFIED-TAG-LIFT |
| `paddingBottom` | 10 | P-PARENT-QUALIFIED-TAG-LIFT |

### 2. Slot-list-driven gaps — 158 of 1102 entries (14%)

158 entries have `source: stage_3_slot_list` — the slot vocabulary expected the attribute but cv2 didn't emit it at all. Distinct from class 1: cv2 doesn't know these slots exist on the emitted blocks.

Likely related to the **868 canonical-slot gap candidates** flagged by `/sgs-update` Task 0.1's `assign-canonical.py` pass. Phase 2 (P-COVERAGE-METRIC-CORE-STYLE + P-COVERAGE-SCOPE-FILTER) makes the coverage% metric honest about this gap class so we can track it; root cause sits with slot vocabulary expansion in `sgs-framework.db` (not on this session's critical path).

### 3. Structural mismatch in 3 composite sections — 3 entries

3 sections (featured-product, gift-section, social-proof) produced `cv2_handled_no_top_level_match`: cv2 fragmented them into child blocks (sgs/label, core/heading, sgs/text, etc.) but couldn't recognise the top-level container as any known SGS block. The emitted_blocks lists suggest these are **composite patterns** (multiple SGS blocks under one parent), not single-block sections.

This is **NOT** a Phase 2/3 fix — it's a pattern-recognition problem that needs either (a) new SGS composite blocks (featured-product, gift-section) or (b) pattern-level matching infrastructure. Mark as Phase 5/6 deferred work; surface as decisions when those sections come up in the iterative loop.

## Headline takeaway

**86% of pixel-parity loss has a single root cause class — cv2 emits the block but fails to lift style attributes.** Phase 2 (honest measurement) + Phase 3 (lift logic fixes) directly attack 1086 of 1107 entries. The 21 remaining entries split between slot-vocabulary gaps (deferred) and 3 composite-section structural mismatches (Phase 5+).

This validates the foundation work in the plan. Phase 5 section ordering: social-proof → ingredients → brand → gift → hero → featured-product → trust-bar.

## Comparison to 2026-05-17 pre-Phase-2A run

| Metric | 2026-05-17 | 2026-05-18 | Delta |
|--------|------------|------------|-------|
| Total entries | 596 | 1107 | +511 |
| Sections covered | 5 (hero/brand/ingredients/trust-bar/header/footer) | 7 (adds social-proof, gift-section, featured-product) | +3 |

The +511 delta is **NOT a regression**. The 2026-05-17 run apparently didn't traverse social-proof / gift-section / featured-product (262+165+81 = 508 of the +511). Phase 2A merged `--auto-section` improvements that expose those sections to cv2 — gaps existed in 2026-05-17 but went uncounted. This is honest-measurement improvement, not new breakage.
